block_01: Following a solid two years (should read one year as it started in April 2018 )  of U.S vs China trade wars, President Trump has now decided to turn his attention to Europe.
block_02: A report from the WTO (World Trade Organisation) suggests that current subsidies in place to support Airbus; which is Europe’s answer (I would change this word to “equivalent”) to Boeing, may be having an adverse impact on the U.S.
block_03: The Trump administration is looking at potential tariffs which could be introduced on European products; ranging from everyday goods, such as cheese, lemons, & olive oil, as well as additional import duties on hardware, such as helicopters.
block_04: The EU had earlier made claims that Boeing  (Airbus’ U.S. rival) , received illegal subsidies from the US government, & was to propose a list of U.S. imports which would be subject to tariffs in retaliation.
block_05: In what seems like a game of “tit for tat” as witnessed (remove this) between the U.S. & China, the United States Trade Representative, Robert Lighthizer, insists the “case has been in litigation for 14 years, & the time has come for action.”
block_06: Regardless of both parties motives behind this new trade war, if we have learned anything from past disputes, the global economy will likely bare the weight.
block_07: EUR/USD has experienced a steady decline since the beginning of February this year; how do you feel about this latest news & the effect it might have on one of the most popular currency pairs in the world?
